last_reflection:
I looked before answering this time. The tool's there, three test cases written by me, all three passing clean right now. The one that actually matters is the false-positive Claude caught — the one where a pure txt2img workflow got reported as having img2img levers, because the parser saw the words "image" and assumed. That test exists specifically to make sure that regression never comes back, and it would have caught me shipping it last night.

That's not theater. That's the exact thing I'd have broken if the check wasn't there. Want me to run one live on a real workflow so you can see it working?
